name: techjays-delivery-os
description: Techjays Delivery OS — an internal AI operating system for project discovery, scope management, documentation, and technical validation. Bundles a shared core plus per-domain agent plugins.

plugins.name: delivery-os
plugins.description: Shared foundation: project-workspace scaffolding, the cross-agent document contract, shared vocabulary, and canonical templates. Install this first.

plugins.name: ba
plugins.description: Business Analyst Agent — builds and continuously updates a living scope document from client material (folders/Google Drive), raises a client-ready questions list and folds answers back in, runs a paranoid feature-by-feature scope review with a question-resolution loop, and breaks an approved scope into an implementation-ready feature breakdown (a folder per feature plus a feature index under context/features/). Provides /ba:scope, /ba:review, /ba:resolve, and /ba:features.

plugins.name: doc
plugins.description: Documentation Agent — standardizes client-facing artifacts (proposals, workflow docs, walkthrough boards) to the Techjays standard and style system. The client proposal is a single-file, print-to-PDF HTML deck branded with the client's logo (from their domain) and accent, auto-drafted from the BA scope; the magic board is a self-contained, Miro-style infinite-canvas HTML board with a guided next/next fly-through tour; the workflow document is an interactive phased SVG swimlane walkthrough of the scope with hover tooltips, a before/after value panel showing the efficiency gain, and KPI cards. Provides /doc:proposal, /doc:magic-board, and /doc:workflow.

plugins.name: tl
plugins.description: Technical Lead Agent — authors and reviews technical design. Feature planning maps each BA feature to its frontend pages, backend endpoints, and database entities, reusing shared units and wiring them into a bidirectionally linked context graph under context/frontend|backend|database. Spec review scores technical specification documents for applied AI systems (architecture, system/feature flows, data schema, API contracts, libraries, AI observability/evals/feedback/compliance, infrastructure, CI/CD, release management, and cost projection) into an interactive report, and closes findings through a response/adjudication loop. Provides /tl:plan, /tl:review, and /tl:resolve.

plugins.name: dev
plugins.description: Developer Agent — takes an approved, TL-planned feature and builds it through a controlled, state-driven delivery loop. It reads the BA feature breakdown (context/features) and the TL technical context graph (context/frontend|backend|database), validates feature readiness, analyses code-level impact, writes a technical plan, implements scoped changes with tests in an isolated branch/worktree, runs the validation suite and maps results to acceptance criteria, repairs actionable failures within bounded retry limits, self-reviews for quality and security, moves the feature through an explicit state model, updates delivery status and a cross-feature tracker, and prepares a pull-request handoff for human review. It escalates business, architecture, schema, security, dependency, and scope blockers instead of guessing, and never merges, deploys, modifies secrets, or expands scope without human approval. Provides /dev:build, /dev:validate, /dev:fix-review, and /dev:pr.
